title: The second altermagnet candidate in organic conductors: $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)$_2$$m$-HOOCC$_6$H$_4$SO$_3$
authors: Kohei Tokura, Takato Masuta, Kazushi Aoyama, Hiroki Akutsu, Yasuhiro Nakazawa, Scott S. Turner
abstract: We have developed a novel BEDT-TTF-based organic conductor, $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)$_2 m$-HOOCC$_6$H$_4$SO$_3$ ($κ$-$m$-SBA), and propose it as a candidate for altermagnet. Tight-binding band calculations of $κ$-$m$-SBA provide a $t'/t$ of 1.01 at 100 K, indicating that the spin structure is closely aligned to an equilateral triangle ($t'/t= 1$). While most $κ$-type BEDT-TTF-based salts become spin liquids due to the spin frustration caused by the triangular lattice, $κ$-$m$-SBA surprisingly shows a weak ferromagnetic transition at $T_\mathrm{N} = 14$ K due to a canted antiferromagnetic (AFM) spin structure. Until recently, $κ$-(BEDT-TTF)$_2$Cu[N(CN)$_2$]Cl ($κ$-Cl) was the only $κ$-type organic conductor known to exhibit this order, and it is also recognized as the first candidate for altermagnetism in organic conductors. This was theoretically predicted by Naka et al. in 2019, who demonstrated that $κ$-type organic conductors can be candidates for altermagnetism if they display such order. Consequently, $κ$-$m$-SBA can be considered the second candidate for altermagnetism in organic conductors. Furthermore, numerical calculations demonstrate a characteristic of altermagnets in $κ$-$m$-SBA, namely spin splitting of energy bands.
summary: The researchers synthesized a new organic conductor, kappa-m-SBA, and identified it as a rare candidate for altermagnetism. By combining experimental measurements with numerical simulations, they demonstrated that the material exhibits spin-split energy bands driven by its specific magnetic ordering. This provides a new platform for studying altermagnetic phenomena in organic molecular crystals.
main_problem: The identification of a new candidate for altermagnetism in organic conductors and explaining why the material kappa-m-SBA exhibits a canted antiferromagnetic transition instead of a spin liquid state.
main_result: The discovery of kappa-m-SBA as the second organic altermagnet candidate, demonstrating characteristic spin-splitting of energy bands due to the interplay of AFM order and anisotropic hopping.
method: The study combines electrocrystallization synthesis, SQUID magnetometry, and four-probe resistivity measurements with first-principles (OpenMX) and tight-binding band calculations using a Hubbard model.
model_or_system: The system is a kappa-type organic conductor, kappa-(BEDT-TTF)2m-HOOCC6H4SO3, characterized by a nearly equilateral triangular lattice and a Hubbard model Hamiltonian including intra-dimer and inter-dimer transfer integrals.
key_observables: Néel temperature (TN = 14 K), magnetic susceptibility, magnetization, electrical resistivity, spin-dependent band splitting, and the frustration parameter (t'/t).
important_parameters: Transfer integrals (t, t', tp, tq), Coulomb interaction (U = 0.54 eV), Mott gap (0.22 eV), and the frustration parameter (t'/t approx 1.01 or 0.80).
main_assumptions: The canted antiferromagnetic structure is approximated as a collinear antiferromagnetic structure to evaluate lattice symmetry-driven altermagnetism.
figures_summary: Figure 5 shows the energy band dispersion in the AFM insulating phase demonstrating spin splitting; Figure S8 illustrates the crystal structure of the anionic layer.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a new organic conductor, describes its synthesis and structural properties, presents magnetic and transport measurements, uses first-principles and tight-binding calculations to analyze the electronic and magnetic ground state, and concludes with the identification of altermagnetic signatures.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
